title: Freeze-dried formulations including nanoparticles and methods of freeze-drying
abstract: Provided are freeze-dried (FD) products of biological materials that have improved stability and reduced risk of collapse. The FD products include nanoparticles that are added to formulations including the biological materials prior to freeze-drying. Also described herein are methods of freeze-drying (FD).
claims: Claims Claims 1. A composition comprising: a biological material; a polyhydroxyl compound; and nanoparticles, wherein the composition is freeze-dried. 2. A composition comprising: a biological material; polyhydroxyl compound; and nanoparticles, wherein the composition is an aqueous solution. 3. A composition comprising: a biological material; polyhydroxyl compound; and nanoparticles, wherein the composition is a solid-state mixture. 4. The composition of any of claims 1-3, further comprising phosphate ions. 5. The composition of any of claims 1-4, wherein the nanoparticles are polymer-stabilized calcium phosphate nanoparticles. 6. The composition of any of claims 1-4, wherein the nanoparticles are starch nanoparticles. 7. The composition of any of claims 1-6, wherein the polyhydroxyl compound is a monosaccharide, a disaccharide, or a polysaccharide. 8. The composition of any of claims 1-6, wherein the polyhydroxyl compound is trehalose. 9. The composition of any of claims 1-8, wherein the biological material is a microorganism. 10. The composition of any of claims 1-8, wherein the biological material is a lactic acid bacteria. 11. The composition of any of claims 1-8, wherein the biological material is a probiotic. 12. The composition of any of claims 1-11, wherein water in the composition is concentrated in the nanoparticles. 13. The composition of claim 2, wherein the nanoparticles constitute between 1- 5% by weight of the aqueous solution. 14. The composition of any of claims 1 and 4-12, wherein the composition is freeze-dried and the water activity a w of the freeze-dried composition is less than 0.2. 15. A method comprising: mixing a biological material, a polyhydroxyl compound, and nanoparticles in an aqueous solution; drying the aqueous solution to form a solid; performing a freeze-drying (FD) protocol to sublimate ice crystals in the solid and form a freeze-dried product. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the nanoparticles are polymer-stabilized calcium phosphate nanoparticles. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the nanoparticles are starch nanoparticles. 18. The method of any of claims 15-17, wherein the polyhydroxyl compound is a monosaccharide, a disaccharide, or a polysaccharide. 19. The method of any of claims 15-18, wherein the polyhydroxyl compound is trehalose. 20. The method of any of claims 15-19, wherein the biological material is a microorganism. 21. The method of any of claims 15-20, wherein the biological material is a lactic acid bacteria. 22. The method of any of claims 15-20, wherein the biological material is a probiotic. 23. The method of any of claims 15-22, wherein water in the composition is concentrated in the nanoparticles. 24. The method of any of claims 15-23, wherein the nanoparticles constitute between 1-5% by weight of the aqueous solution. 25. The method of any of claims 15-24, wherein the water activity a w of the freeze-dried composition is less than 0.2. 26. The method of any of claims 15-24, wherein the water activity a w of the freeze-dried composition is less than 0.15.
